title: Russia Launched Record 6,800+ Drones and Missiles at Ukraine in April 2026, Data Shows
summary: Ukraine's air force data reveals Russia launched at least 6,804 long-range drones and missiles during April 2026, surpassing the previous monthly record set in March and averaging 222 drones per night, while intensifying a new tactic of daytime mass strikes.
contents:
## Record Russian Aerial Bombardment

### Scale of Attacks
Ukraine's air force reported that Russia launched at least 6,804 long-range drones and missiles into Ukraine during April 2026 — comprising 6,663 drones and 141 missiles — marking the most intense month of long-range aerial attacks since the conflict began in February 2022.

### Nightly Average
The data suggests Ukraine faced a nightly average of approximately 222 drones and four missiles throughout April. The single largest attack occurred on the night of April 15, when Moscow launched 703 munitions — 659 drones and 44 missiles — killing at least 19 people.

### Tactical Shift to Daytime Strikes
Russia significantly escalated daytime long-range drone attacks during April, abandoning the previous pattern of exclusively overnight strikes. The Institute for the Study of War (ISW) assessed this tactic as designed to "more heavily target civilians and civilian infrastructure including public and open-space areas, especially as warmer weather arrives and more Ukrainians spend time outside."

### Interception Rate
Ukrainian defenses intercepted or suppressed nearly 88% of incoming drones and more than 62% of missiles, according to air force data. Kyiv has marketed its drone defense expertise internationally, with Ukrainian interceptor systems now deployed in Gulf states to counter Iranian drones.

### Cross-Border Escalation
The drone exchanges prompted multiple incursions into NATO member airspace, including Romania, Poland, Lithuania, and Latvia. Poland formally invoked NATO's Article 4 over Russian drone violations, initiating official consultations. NATO aircraft regularly scramble in border nations to respond to Russian drone activity along Ukraine's western frontiers.

### Ukrainian Counterstrikes
Ukraine simultaneously intensified deep strikes against Russian energy infrastructure. SBU drones hit the Lukoil refinery in Perm (1,500 km from Ukraine), a refinery in Orenburg's Orsk city, and repeatedly struck the Tuapse oil terminal on the Black Sea coast — triggering a local state of emergency, environmental contamination, and school closures.
